publishedAbstract: In some instances during troubleshooting, there may be a pod that is in a state of `Terminating` indefinitely. After some time has passed and it's clear that the pod will not terminate, users often use the `$ oc delete pod-n--force` command to resolve this. However, in the event the pod is hosting a volume (PV/PVC) that is in AccessMode Read Write Once (RWO); this will most likely cause a multi-attach error. multi-attach errors can be difficult to resolve once they've been established and may require multiple nodes to be rebooted. The steps below can be used to prevent this. **Related Articles:** [How to safely reboot an OCS/ODF 4 node](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/6495631)
publishedTitle: A Pod Using the "ceph-rbd" StorageClass with PVC in AccessMode "RWO" is Stuck Terminating and the --force Option Must be Used - "Multi-Attach error"
